subtasks.subtask: Import auth components từ frontend hoặc create locally
After successful login, get session từ Supabase
Session automatically stored via chrome.storage adapter (Story 13.1)
Check auth state on side panel open
Display user info if logged in
Hide login form if logged in
Test auth state persists after side panel close
Test auth state persists after browser restart
Test auth state loads on side panel open

acceptanceCriteria.ac: Login UI trong side panel (reuse frontend auth components)
Auth token stored in chrome.storage after login
Auth state persists across browser sessions

doc.title: Epic 13: API Integration & Authentication
doc.section: Story 13.3: Authentication Flow in Side Panel

doc.title: Story 13.1: Chrome Storage Adapter for Supabase
doc.snippet: Supabase client available. Auth state persists via chrome.storage adapter.

doc.title: Story 13.2: API Client Adaptation
doc.snippet: API client ready. Token retrieval from chrome.storage works.

doc.title: Story 12.2: Popup Layout & Structure
doc.snippet: Side panel layout ready. Login UI can be integrated into side panel.

dependencies.prerequisite: Story 13.2: API client adaptation must be complete
Story 12.2: Side panel layout must be created
Story 13.1: Supabase client với storage adapter must be complete

constraints.constraint: Login UI must reuse frontend auth components if possible
Auth state must persist across browser sessions
Logout must clear auth state correctly
Side panel must show logged-in state when auth state is available

testing.test: Test login UI displays trong side panel
Test logged-in state trong side panel
Test auth persistence across side panel sessions
